results.name: 05_conflicting_requirements
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: The model captures top-level requirements for a physical system with constraints on shape, material, and mass.
- Operational domain: Generic mechanical/structural product domain; no specific industry context is stated.
- Model scope: Very small requirement-only fragment, not a complete system model. It contains requirement definitions and requirement usages only.
- Known scope limitations: No behavioural model, no interfaces, no allocations, no parametrics, no state machines, no ports, and no quantified material property calculations are present. Checks requiring such content are assessed only insofar as the requirement text permits; otherwise they are skipped with rationale.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts - Examined the explicit numeric limits in the requirement text; no direct contradictory numeric bounds are stated, and the only numeric relationship present is described as mutually consistent.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts - No behavioural elements (states, transitions, activities, triggers, guards, or timing sequences) are present in this requirement-only fragment, so no behavioural conflict can be evidenced from the model text.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts - No interfaces, ports, flows, connections, or protocol specifications are present in the model fragment, so this check is not applicable.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Requirement-Level Conflicts - Reviewed the four requirement statements together; the model explicitly adds `GeometryMaterialMassConsistency` stating that shape, material, and mass "shall be jointly satisfied," which acts as an explicit resolution note rather than introducing a contradiction.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts - No functional, logical-to-physical, or software-to-hardware allocations are present in the model, so allocation conflict checks are not applicable.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts - No safety requirements, safety constraints, or functional behaviour requirements are present, so no safety/functional conflict can be evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Priority & Resolution - For the only potentially tensioned set of requirements (shape, material, mass), the model includes an explicit resolution requirement `GeometryMaterialMassResolution : GeometryMaterialMassConsistency;` documenting intended joint satisfaction, and no unresolved conflict is visible in the text.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| None | 1.0 | HIGH | No conflicting requirements or constraints were evidenced in this model fragment. |

Overall Assessment Score: 1.0  
Model Status: Good - minor improvements only
